perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information within 30 days of the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the return of Confidential Information. Initially, materials must be returned 'immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing,' but now they must be returned 'within 30 days of the termination of this agreement' regardless of any specific request. This creates ambiguity about when the Receiving Party is obligated to return the materials and undermines the Disclosing Party's control over their information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obliga tions of Receiving Party . Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information within 30 days of the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to return Confidential Information 'immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing'. The changed text alters this to 'within 30 days of the termination of this agreement'. This creates a direct contradiction because the Receiving Party no longer has to comply with the Disclosing Party's request to return the information immediately, but instead can wait until 30 days after termination. This is an inconsistency that leads to an in-text contradiction, as defined in Perturbation Definition 5, because it provides conflicting deadlines. The contradicted text contains the changed text regarding the 30 day return period, demonstrating the inconsistency that now exists in the document.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings. This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings. This Agreement may be amended by mutual agreement, including oral agreements confirmed by email.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction regarding how the contract can be amended. The original text states that amendments must be 'in writing signed by both parties'. The modified text contradicts this by stating that amendments can be done by 'oral agreements confirmed by email'. This introduces uncertainty over which method of amendment is legally binding and could lead to disputes over changes to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Integration . This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings. This Agreement may be amended by mutual agreement, including oral agreements confirmed by email.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement by allowing amendments through 'oral agreements confirmed by email', which directly contradicts the original text (and also the contradicted text which is the same as the changed text) that requires amendments to be 'in writing signed by both parties'. This creates a clear inconsistency, making it unclear which method is legally binding, thus a strong in-text contradiction exists as it meets the definition of inconsistencies and the explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: Employee is provided notice that an individual shall not be held criminally or civilly liable under any federal or state trade secret law for the disclosure of a trade secret that is made (i) in confidence to a federal, state, or local government official, either directly or indirectly, or to an attorney; and (ii) solely for the purpose of reporting or investigating a suspected violation of law; or is made in a complaint or other document filed in a lawsuit or other proceeding, if such filing is made under seal. An individual who files a lawsuit for retaliation by an employer for reporting a suspected violation of law may disclose the trade secret to the attorney of the individual and use the trade secret information in the court proceeding, if the individual (i) files any document containing the trade secret under seal; and (ii) does not disclose the trade secret, except pursuant t o court order. This Agreement and each party's obligations shall be binding on the representatives, assigns and successors of such party. Each party has signed this Agreement through its authorized representative.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee is provided notice that an individual shall not be held criminally or civilly liable under any federal or state trade secret law for the disclosure of a trade secret that is made (i) in confidence to a federal, state, or local government official, either directly or indirectly, or to an attorney; and (ii) solely for the purpose of reporting or investigating a suspected violation of law; or is made in a complaint or other document filed in a lawsuit or other proceeding, if such filing is made under seal. An individual who files a lawsuit for retaliation by an employer for reporting a suspected violation of law may disclose the trade secret to the attorney of the individual and use the trade secret information in the court proceeding, if the individual (i) files any document containing the trade secret under seal; and (ii) does not disclose the trade secret, except pursuant to court order. This Agreement and each party's obligations shall be binding on the representatives, assigns and successors of such party, except for any subsidiaries of the parties, which shall not be bound by this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction by excluding subsidiaries from being bound by the agreement, while simultaneously stating that the agreement is binding on 'successors of such party.' Subsidiaries are often considered successors or related entities, so the exclusion creates uncertainty. It contradicts the general binding nature of the agreement on successors and introduces ambiguity regarding the obligations of entities related to the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notice of Immunity . Employee is provided notice that an individual shall not be held criminally or civilly liable under any federal or state trade secret law for the disclosure of a trade secret that is made (i) in confidence to a federal, state, or local government official, either directly or indirectly, or to an attorney; and (ii) solely for the purpose of reporting or investigating a suspected violation of law; or is made in a complaint or other document filed in a lawsuit or other proceeding, if such filing is made under seal. An individual who files a lawsuit for retaliation by an employer for reporting a suspected violation of law may disclose the trade secret to the attorney of the individual and use the trade secret information in the court proceeding, if the individual (i) files any document containing the trade secret under seal; and (ii) does not disclose the trade secret, except pursuant t o court order. This Agreement and each party's obligations shall be binding on the representatives, assigns and successors of such party, except for any subsidiaries of the parties, which shall not be bound by this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception stating that subsidiaries are not bound by the agreement, while the original text states that the agreement is binding on 'successors of such party.' Subsidiaries are often considered successors, creating a direct conflict within the text. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, further solidifying the in-text contradiction introduced by the exclusion of subsidiaries.
